messages.content:
Which of the following is a priority area for achieving energy efficiency and net-zero for industry?


Digital asset management

Green jobs

Renewable heating fuels such as green hydrogen

Levelized cost of energy (LCOE)
Renewable heating fuels such as green hydrogen is a priority area for achieving energy efficiency and net-zero for industry.

In which of the following measures to curb climate change are most organizations still lagging behind?


Investing in insurance against climate-related risks

Committing to net-zero

Meeting the minimum robustness criteria set out by the United Nations (UN) Race to Zero campaign

Digitizing their entire value chain with advanced technologies
Committing to net-zero is an area in which most organizations are still lagging behind in measures to curb climate change.

Which of the following types of greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions fall under the category of Scope 3 emissions?


GHG emissions that companies generate from purchased energy

GHG emissions that companies offset from their owned and controlled resources

GHG emissions that companies generate from their owned and controlled resources

GHG emissions that companies generate in their value chain, both upstream and downstream
GHG emissions that companies generate in their value chain, both upstream and downstream, fall under the category of Scope 3 emissions.

Which of the following priority areas for technology and transformation can accelerate the delivery of modernized and enhanced electricity, gas, and water infrastructures?


Clean power

Connected lower-carbon infrastructure (CLCI)

Grid technology

Electric mobility (e-mobility)

Connected lower-carbon infrastructure (CLCI) is a priority area for technology and transformation that can accelerate the delivery of modernized and enhanced electricity, gas, and water infrastructures.

Which of the following aspects should organizations prioritize in order to make an inclusive transition to green jobs?


Supporting responsible leadership

Reducing greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions

Aligning employees’ values to sustainability

Retaining the existing skillsets and roles
Supporting responsible leadership is an aspect that organizations should prioritize in order to make an inclusive transition to green jobs.

The supply chains of organizations worldwide are a huge contributor to overall greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions. Which of the following categories of emissions do organizations need to address on priority?


Scope 2 emissions

Scope 3 emissions

Scope 0 emissions

Scope 1 emissions
Organizations need to prioritize addressing Scope 3 emissions which refer to GHG emissions generated in their value chain, including upstream and downstream emissions.

Accenture is a strategic partner with the World Economic Forum (WEF). Which of the following are two key initiatives that Accenture and WEF are driving together to accelerate net-zero globally?


Water Neutrality and Cloud-Based Emissions Reduction

City Sprint and Sustainability By Tech

Grid Technology and E-Mobility

Net-Zero Carbon Cities and Industrial Clusters
The two key initiatives that Accenture and WEF are driving together to accelerate net-zero globally are Net-Zero Carbon Cities and Industrial Clusters.

A group of companies come together and draw up a contract to deploy and use a common infrastructure for capturing carbon generated in their value chains. Which of the following elements of the net-zero transition framework does this arrangement address?


Decarbonize Industry

Recovery and Removals

Reinvent Demand and Behavior

Decarbonize Energy Systems

Decarbonize Transport

This arrangement addresses the 'Decarbonize Industry' element of the net-zero transition framework because it involves the deployment of infrastructure that captures carbon generated in the value chains of companies, thereby reducing emissions from industrial processes.

What is the “City Sprint” process?


A way to decarbonize, generate new jobs in underserved areas, and deliver vital benefits like better air quality and health.

A series of workshops involving business and public service leaders in which city stakeholders can evaluate and prioritize net-zero solutions for near-term implementation in their city.

A solution that helps build an ecosystem where participants can share data and promote water reuse within and between industries.

A method to analyze Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions across multiple industries by considering target-setting approaches and measurement standards and methodologies.

"City Sprint" is a series of workshops involving business and public service leaders in which city stakeholders can evaluate and prioritize net-zero solutions for near-term implementation in their city.

Why are organizations looking to improve their ability to measure and report on their environmental, social, and governance (ESG) commitments?


To meet their re-financing requirements through avenues such as green bonds

To make a more sustainable use of innovative technology

To deal with their supply chain disruptions

To ensure a fair and ethical transition to net-zero greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions

Organizations are looking to improve their ability to measure and report on their environmental, social, and governance (ESG) commitments to ensure a fair and ethical transition to net-zero greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions. This includes meeting stakeholder expectations and regulatory requirements, building and maintaining a reputation for sustainability, and demonstrating a commitment to social responsibility and accountability. It can also help attract investment, including green bonds, and better manage ESG risks and opportunities in the supply chain.

Talent is one of the key areas of Accenture’s 360° Value framework. How does the net-zero transition deliver impact in this area?


When a company takes action on achieving its net-zero transition goals, it attracts fresh talent who are driven by their personal sustainability agendas.

An increasing number of millennials refuse to work with companies that do not have a strong sustainability and responsibility policy.

Many companies are keen to hire fresh talent from low-income communities to ensure they are fairly represented.

Net-zero investments, which have a global reach and span several decades, can shape portfolio strategies that significantly improve productivity and nurture talent.

When a company takes action on achieving its net-zero transition goals, it attracts fresh talent who are driven by their personal sustainability agendas. The net-zero transition can create new job opportunities in areas such as clean energy and sustainable infrastructure, while also encouraging skill-building and training programs for existing employees. Additionally, a company's commitment to sustainability can enhance employee satisfaction and retention, as well as its reputation as a responsible employer.

How does the System Value Framework ensure a practical and ethical transition to net-zero emissions?


The framework guides organizations to prioritize profitability over the social value of net-zero.

The framework helps accelerate the journey toward net-zero transitions.

The framework shifts the political and commercial focus beyond social and environmental value to include cost.

The framework evaluates the economic, environmental, social, and technical outcomes of potential energy solutions.

The System Value Framework evaluates the economic, environmental, social, and technical outcomes of potential energy solutions, thereby ensuring a practical and ethical transition to net-zero emissions. The framework recognizes that a net-zero transition requires the consideration of social and environmental factors alongside economic and technical factors, and aims to balance these considerations to deliver the most sustainable outcomes for all stakeholders involved. It helps organizations to identify opportunities to generate value beyond traditional profitability metrics, such as job creation, innovation, and reduced emissions.

How can companies help reinvent demand and behavior to aid the transition to net-zero greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions?


By nudging their employees to act more sustainably

By making more sustainable consumption choices

By offering products and services with a lower carbon footprint

By forging partnerships to decarbonize their value chains

Companies can help reinvent demand and behavior to aid the transition to net-zero greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions by offering products and services with a lower carbon footprint. By providing low-carbon options for customers to choose from, companies can help shift consumer behavior toward more sustainable consumption patterns. This can include developing sustainable products and services, as well as providing education and information to help customers make more informed choices. Additionally, companies can also work with partners across their value chain to reduce emissions and promote more sustainable practices.

One of the ways in which governments can help the net-zero transition is through _______ that give businesses the certainty to commit and make investments in new products or capabilities.


partnerships to decarbonize companies’ value chains

products and services with a lower carbon footprint

clear policy frameworks

Clear policy frameworks can help the net-zero transition by giving businesses the certainty to commit and make investments in new products or capabilities. When governments establish clear and consistent policies that support the transition to net-zero emissions, it can provide the regulatory certainty and long-term signaling that businesses need to plan and invest for the future. This can include policies such as carbon pricing, emissions standards, energy efficiency targets, and renewable energy mandates, among others. By creating a supportive policy environment, governments can help create a level playing field for businesses and enable them to invest in low-carbon solutions with greater confidence.

What is the purpose of the 360° Value framework?


The framework evaluates the economic and social outcomes of potential energy solutions while delivering carbon reduction.

The framework allows for the creation of a digital integrated system that promotes System Value.

The framework is designed to help clients to transform and reinvent their businesses, reskill their employees, and become more sustainable.

The framework is designed to estimate the cost-related outcomes of energy solutions.

The purpose of the 360° Value framework is to help clients transform and reinvent their businesses, reskill their employees, and become more sustainable. The framework takes a holistic view of value creation, including economic, environmental, and social factors, and seeks to maximize value across all dimensions. It provides a structured approach for stakeholders to identify opportunities to create value beyond traditional measures of profitability and efficiency. The framework also highlights the importance of ecosystems and partnerships in delivering sustainable outcomes, and provides a set of tools and methodologies to drive change and innovation.
